description.value:
Tonttu do not fight large things directly if they can arrange for large things to trip, dangle, and embarrass themselves. The snare-art is taught quietly, passed through households under the name "pest-work," and only the very inattentive fail to notice that the same knot that catches a rat would take a man's ankle just as cleanly.

This project produces a set of six snares following traditional Tonttu trap-making methods. The snares are constructed from lightweight cord, bent wood, and a trigger pin of compressed bark — small, low-profile, and very patient. When set by a hero with knowledge of the technique, each snare triggers on a creature of Small size or larger that crosses the trigger-line. A caught creature is immediately restrained until they free themselves. The snares can be reset and reused unless damaged.

Rules Note: A set of 6 Tonttu snares. Any creature of Small size or larger that crosses a deployed snare's trigger must succeed on an Agility test (difficulty set at time of crafting, default Medium) or become Restrained until they free themselves with a Might test.

system.prerequisites: Fine wire or braided cord (6 lengths), bent green-wood stakes, compressed bark trigger-pins, basic knot-working tools.
system.projectSource: Tonttu Trap-Setter's Guide — a household tradition, passed verbally with occasional gesture-demonstrations. No written version is considered authoritative.

yield.display: A set of 6 Tonttu snares: triggered by creatures Small or larger; Restrained until freed with a Might test. Reusable.
